rationale: The citing paper uses Ashok, Dillon, and Yuan (2002) repeatedly in its core literature positioning, not just in a passing list. It identifies that paper as a representative ICLV model in marketing, discusses specific properties of its setup (attitudinal latent variables, SEM-based binary choice, correlation among attitudes), and includes it in a structured model-comparison table. The new model is framed as addressing limitations of that prior ICLV stream, so the citation materially informs the conceptual and methodological setup of the paper.
relation_description: Burke et al. use Ashok et al. (2002) as an important prior benchmark for integrating latent variables into choice models. Their contribution is positioned as moving beyond that foundation from individual-level attitudinal ICLV formulations to multiattribute, multiple-mediator benefit-based choice modeling.
